channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Secure Medallion Architecture Pattern on Azure Databricks (Part II)
item.description: Disclaimer: The views in this article are my own and do not represent Microsoft or Databricks. This article is part of a series focused on deploying a secure Medallion Architecture. The series follows a top-down approach , beginning with a high-level architectural perspective and gradually drilling...
item.dc:creator: mscagliola

item.title: How can I delete or clear clipboard history on mac?
item.description: Hi guys,Bit of a weird question, but how do you actually delete clipboard history on a Mac? I copied some sensitive stuff earlier and now I want to make sure it's gone completely.I know macOS clipboard works differently from Windows, so I'm not sure if there's a built-in way to clear it or if I...
item.dc:creator: Axton-X

item.title: New Steam Controller can't control PC on Bluetooth until I first select Steam
item.description: Edit for the people who didn't understand: Steam is running. Steam Controller does literally nothing until I select Steam with my mouse. Then the controller works.Original post: Windows 10, I have my puck attached to a different computer and until I can get a second controller this is the setup I...
item.dc:creator: JacquelineSom

item.title: Need help with hp victus 15-fb0131ax
item.description: so, i was tweaking settings with help of copilot, when i was trying to unhide hybrid sleep and few settings i accidentally hidden , i trusted copilot method blindly and changed attributes of all settings under power settings, which lead to error and blank window when opening advanced power settings...
item.dc:creator: Arthurew

item.title: Giving the Copilot SDK Agent a "hardware-level helmet" using Kata microVM on AKS
item.description: A Moment That Made Me Pause I was recently building an Agent service with the GitHub Copilot SDK. After getting it up and running, I went back through the execution logs and something jumped out at me: In a single conversation turn, the Agent had executed a shell command, read several files, and...
item.dc:creator: kinfey

item.title: How are you running 1:1 meetings inside Teams without it turning into a mess?
item.description: Right now were using a shared OneNote for agendas and action items but honestly nobody updates it and half the time the meeting turns into a random chat with no structure. I tried using Planner tasks as a workaround but thats clunky because theres no real connection between the task and the meeting...
item.dc:creator: ChrisLeider

item.title: Comment télécharger des vidéos YouTube en masse en 2026
item.description: Bonjour à tous,Je cherche une bonne méthode pour télécharger plusieurs vidéos YouTube en même temps sur mon PC Windows en 2026. J’ai beaucoup de playlists et de longues vidéos à sauvegarder pour les regarder hors ligne.J’ai testé quelques sites web, mais ils sont souvent lents, pleins de pubs ou...
item.dc:creator: Damk

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | April 2026
item.description: April brings Foundry Local GA for local AI development, GPT-5.5 model support with Tier 5 and Tier 6 default quota in Microsoft Foundry, new tracing paths for Microsoft Agent Framework and hosted agents, CodeAct with Hyperlight for sandboxed agent code execution, expanded monitoring and continuous...
item.dc:creator: Nick Brady

item.title: Introducing PII Shield: A Privacy Proxy for Every LLM Call
item.description: Why do we need a utility like PII Shield? Let’s start with an uncomfortable truth: modern AI systems are swimming in sensitive data - often without us fully realizing the risks we’re taking. The data is sensitive. Names, emails, phone numbers, credit cards, IBANs, SSNs, Aadhaar, PAN, Driving...
item.dc:creator: vikas_gautam

item.title: Will I be able to install Windows 10 on my computer?
item.description: I'm a bit at a loss. I don't know what to do. Why did the installation of Windows 10 fail on my computer?
item.dc:creator: Asaoniu

item.title: I ascended. My PC is now a god, and Windows 11 is a forgotten mortal
item.description: For years, I was a Windows slave. A serf to the whims of Bill Gates. I put up with the forced updates that always hit five minutes before a big presentation. I tolerated the bloatware that ate my RAM like it was a Thanksgiving dinner. And I even drank the Windows 11 Kool-Aid, thinking a fresh coat...
item.dc:creator: Abigailop

item.title: My computer is old (OLD) - Will Windows 11 run well?
item.description: I know it looks like trolling/ragebait, but please don't disregard it as that. I'm poor. This is my only computer. As you can see it is top 2025 technology /sIt is running with an HDD only and the graphics are Intel G41 express chipset.Two years ago I used to use Windows 10 on this same machine,...
item.dc:creator: Nicoleep

item.title: Modernizing Azure Landing Zone Deployments: From Terraform Scripts to GHCP-Driven Engineering
item.description: The code nobody wants to write anymore Most Azure Landing Zone (ALZ) deployments we see today still follow the same pattern they did a few years ago. Engineers manually write Terraform modules, copy networking blocks from older repos, tweak management group hierarchies, and wire pipelines step by...
item.dc:creator: gurkirat

item.title: Azure Arc AKS Explained: Run Kubernetes Beyond Azure Cloud
item.description: Modern enterprises are no longer running workloads only inside a centralized cloud environment. Applications today operate across: On-premises datacenters Remote branch offices Manufacturing plants Retail stores Edge locations Hybrid infrastructure While Kubernetes has become the standard for...
item.dc:creator: mohit-kanojia

item.title: Action required: Kerberos RC4 hardening may affect Azure Files Active Directory Domain Services
item.description: A Windows security hardening change beginning in April 2026 updates default Kerberos encryption behavior and may impact customers using Azure Files with Active Directory Domain Services (AD DS) authentication over SMB. If you created Azure Files shares prior to 2023, or chose RC4 encryption for...
item.dc:creator: grace_kim

item.title: How to Secure Azure Databricks without Public Exposure using WAF + Private Endpoints
item.description: While first thing that comes up to mind is that lets configure IP Access List with keeping Azure Databricks in Hybrid Connectivity. This approach is technically doable, but not the best approach for organizations which follows Zero Trust Architecture Framework. This is where organizations has to...
item.dc:creator: FaizaanMerchant

item.title: Introducing the Azure Resource Manager MCP Server!
item.description: We're super excited to announce the public preview of the Azure Resource Manager MCP Server! This is a remote MCP server that provides tools to give AI agents first-class access to Azure infrastructure operations through Azure Resource Manager (ARM). AI agents can now be equipped with tools to...
item.dc:creator: stevenbucher

item.title: Announcing savings plan for databases: flexible savings for modern, evolving workloads
item.description: As organizations modernize their data platforms, database environments are constantly changing. Teams migrate between services, scale across regions, and evolve architectures to support new applications and AI driven workloads. But optimizing costs in these environments can be challenging,...
item.dc:creator: kyleikeda

item.title: Azure CLI on macOS: Upcoming Installation Changes
item.description: From Homebrew Core to a new installation model for macOS Over the past few years, Azure CLI usage on macOS has continued to grow—across individual developers, open source contributors, and large enterprises. As adoption increases, so do expectations around security, reliability, and enterprise...
item.dc:creator: Alex-wdy

item.title: Announcing the AI Dev Days Hackathon winners
item.description: 🏆 Winning projects 🏆 Grand Prize : Build AI Applications & Agents using Microsoft AI Platform and Tools Quipu: AI Early Warning for Peruvian Municipalities (Peru)Quipu helps Peruvian municipalities spot early signals of natural disasters and get alerts out faster. It uses seven collaborating AI...
item.dc:creator: osmancokakoglu

item.title: BYOD devices can't launch Windows 365 PC because of device compliance check during CA policy check.
item.description: We have a device compliance policy for all cloud apps. We would like to allow personal (BYOD) devices to be able to connect to Windows 365 Cloud PC. In the sign in logs we see the failures for application "Windows 365 Client" app id 4fb5cc57-dbbc-4cdc-9595-748adff5f414. We can't exclude that...
item.dc:creator: wcaetano

item.title: Secure the moments attackers target: onboarding, access requests, and account recovery
item.description: High assurance identity verification is no longer limited to regulated industries or edge cases. It is increasingly becoming a baseline requirement for scenarios like remote onboarding, account recovery, and access to sensitive resources. Microsoft tracks over 600 million identity attacks every...
item.dc:creator: AnkurPatel

item.title: Announcing general availability of confidential computing for Azure Service Bus Premium
item.description: Today we are excited to announce the general availability of confidential computing for Azure Service Bus Premium. With this capability, your Service Bus namespace processes messages inside hardware-based trusted execution environments (TEEs), preventing unauthorized access to data while it is...
item.dc:creator: EldertGrootenboer

item.title: Azure Service Bus Premium now offers 99.99% SLA in all Availability Zone regions
item.description: Today we are excited to announce an update to the Azure Service Bus Service Level Agreement. Starting May 1, 2026, all Premium namespaces deployed in regions with Availability Zone support will receive a 99.99% uptime SLA. This applies regardless of whether partitioning is enabled on the namespace....
item.dc:creator: EldertGrootenboer

item.title: Build a secure identity foundation: 5 webinars to strengthen access management with Microsoft Entra
item.description: To avoid gaps in protection, organizations need to fully deploy and scale core identity protections–from phishing-resistant authentication to Conditional Access policies to risk remediation and resiliency–across cloud and hybrid deployments. That’s why we’re launching the Secure identity foundation...
item.dc:creator: Melanie_Maynes

item.title: Data Warehouse Monitor: A Unified Monitoring Experience for Fabric Data Warehouse (Preview)
item.description: Authored by: Mariya Ali - Program Manager II and Twinkle Cyril - Senior Product Manager
item.dc:creator: TwinkleCyril

item.title: Edge and Copilot App - Error code: STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED
item.description: I am running Windows 11 Pro - Insider Preview - Version 25H2 (OS Build 26300-8376)My "Copilot app" from the Windows Store started giving me that error code, and Edge stopped working completely, unable to access anything at all, and shows that error on the screen. Windows support won't even look...
item.dc:creator: ryanokjr

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: RSAC 2026
item.description: Security is entering a new era, one defined by explosive data growth, increasingly sophisticated threats, and the rise of AI-enabled operations. To keep pace, security teams need an AI-powered approach to collect, reason over, and act on security data at scale. At RSA Conference 2026 (RSAC), we’re...
item.dc:creator: spalani

item.title: GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with OSS contributions
item.description: Learn how to find opportunities to contribute to the open source community. The post GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with OSS contributions appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Kedasha Kerr

item.title: Stripe Events + Azure Event Grid: Now Generally Available
item.description: Stripe events give developers real-time visibility into everything happening in their Stripe accounts — payments, subscriptions, refunds, disputes, and more. With Azure Event Grid as a destination for Stripe events, now Generally Available, teams can route those signals directly into Azure and...
item.dc:creator: robece

item.title: What happened to Screen Capture in PC Manager?
item.description: The feature I used the most was the screen capture. It was quick and intuitive. After a recent update it's gone replaced by something called Circle to Act.How do I get it back?
item.dc:creator: BobHatcher1055

item.title: Does the screen look very bright at 100% brightness in Windows 11, but noticeably dimmer at 99%?
item.description: When adjusting the screen brightness, going from 99% to 100% suddenly makes the screen very bright; it feels like there’s a big jump between the two levels rather than a smooth transition. I’m wondering if this is a Windows 11 issue or if it’s caused by hardware or driver settings.
item.dc:creator: ZacharyRobinson

item.title: What is the GameTurbo power plan that suddenly appeared in Windows 11?
item.description: I found a power plan called GameTurbo in the Power Options menu that I've never seen before. I'd like to know what this plan does, where it comes from, and whether it's recommended.
item.dc:creator: AlejandroSmith

item.title: For a new computer with a 2TB SSD running Windows 11, what is the recommended size for the C drive?
item.description: My new computer comes with a 2TB SSD that hasn’t been partitioned yet. A friend suggested reserving 150–300 GB for the C drive, but I used Windows 7 for eight years with only 50 GB allocated to the C drive and still had 20 GB left. Does Windows 11 really need that much space on the C drive? I’d...
item.dc:creator: BenjaminLiu

item.title: The Windows 11 Photos app cannot display the full image when viewing landscape-oriented photos
item.description: I just started using Windows 11, and I noticed that when I run a slideshow in the new Photos app, landscape-oriented images aren’t displayed in full (part of the image is automatically cropped), while portrait-oriented images display normally. Is this a settings issue? How can I fix it?
item.dc:creator: WilliamHuang

item.title: Scaling GitHub Advanced Security in Azure DevOps with a single reusable YAML template
item.description: Scaling GitHub Advanced Security in Azure DevOps with a single reusable YAML template Managing security scanning across dozens of repositories can quickly become complex—especially when each repository uses different languages, frameworks, and infrastructure patterns. In our environment, we needed...
item.dc:creator: Paulams732

item.title: How to do I get my hard drive back?
item.description: I installed windows 11 using windows installation media but now my hard drive is not appearing how do I get it back? It's encrypted with bitlocker and I don't know the security key :(
item.dc:creator: Chandlere

item.title: Windows PC slow after leaving off for months
item.description: So I just got back from school for the summer after having not used my custom built PC for 3-4 months and now windows runs EXTREMELY poorly. Basic stuff like web browsers crash on opening like 50% of the time, it randomly locks up and freezes, I get tons of lag on the desktop screen with nothing...
item.dc:creator: Tomimys

item.title: Window defender not working
item.description: My window defender not working can anyone help me please ?, I just reinstall window 11 at pc store and its not working so i think its not malware problem. 😭
item.dc:creator: Ambrosenb

item.title: Create repositories on the go with GitHub Mobile
item.description: You can now create new repositories directly from the GitHub Mobile app. On iOS, start from either Home or your Profile, tap the + button, and choose Create repository. On… The post Create repositories on the go with GitHub Mobile appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: After resetting the Windows 11 Security Center, it freezes for about a minute before crashing
item.description: I was unable to open the Security Center earlier, so I reset my computer. After the reset, the Security Center opened, but every time I open it, it freezes on the initial screen for about a minute before crashing automatically. I’ve already tried resetting and repairing the Security Center in...
item.dc:creator: ZacharyRobinson

item.title: The Xbox Mode in the Windows 11 KB5083631 update is completely different from previous tests.
item.description: Microsoft has once again failed to deliver on its promise. The so-called “Xbox Mode” in the KB5083631 update is completely different from the Full-Screen Experience (FSE) I previously tested on my laptop.Previously, FSE was fully functional:You could choose any app as the “dashboard” (such as the...
item.dc:creator: ArthurZhang

item.title: After waking from sleep in Windows 11, the screen goes black and the system shuts down immediately
item.description: The computer is a Lenovo model running Windows 11. After waking briefly from sleep mode, the screen quickly goes black, the power cuts out, and the system forces a reboot to the startup screen. The after-sales service team has inspected the hardware and confirmed that there are no issues. I have...
item.dc:creator: NoahPeterson

item.title: After the Windows 11 25H2 update, I can t use any internet-connected software other than the browser
item.description: After updating to Windows 10 version 25H2, the system indicates that the network connection is working normally, but only the browser can access the internet; all other software that requires an internet connection is unusable. A technician came to troubleshoot the issue and ruled out problems with...
item.dc:creator: MateoJohnson

item.title: Teams Migration for New Private Channels Stalls
item.description: Microsoft began an automated background process to move Teams private channels to a new infrastructure earlier this year. Tenants should check the state of the migration as reported by the Get-TenantPrivateChannelMigrationStatus cmdlet because it is possible that the migration is stalled and...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: From Test Cases to Trusted Automation: Scaling Enterprise Quality with GitHub Copilot
item.description: Automation First, But Trust Is Earned Enterprise QA teams today are automation‑led by default. Regression suites run daily, API tests validate integrations, and UI automation protects critical workflows. Yet, many teams still struggle with: Automation suites that lag behind changing requirements...
item.dc:creator: SagarPatra

item.title: Understanding the deployment quota limitation (800) Error in Azure Bicep and ARM Deployments
item.description: Understanding deployment quota limitation (800) Error in Azure Deployments (Bicep/ARM) Introduction When working with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Azure Bicep or ARM templates, deployment failures are a common part of day-to-day operations—especially in large-scale enterprise environments....
item.dc:creator: ranjan_ashish

item.title: Configure DNS forwarding for Azure NetApp Files
item.description: This post has been written with the collaboration of Rizul Khanna Applies to: Azure NetApp Files — SMB, dual-protocol, and NFSv4.1 Kerberos volumes deployed in hub-spoke or Azure Virtual WAN topologies using an external private DNS forwarder. Overview Azure NetApp Files (ANF) has a hard dependency...
item.dc:creator: mkachare

item.title: Apply Copy Fail and DirtyFrag CVE mitigations at-scale using Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager
item.description: See how to apply mitigations for CVE-2026-31431 (Copy Fail) and CVE-2026-43284 / CVE-2026-43500 (DirtyFrag) across AKS clusters using Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager with a DaemonSet and node image upgrades.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Turning GitHub Copilot into a “Best Practices Coach” with Copilot Spaces + a Markdown Knowledge Base
item.description: Why Copilot Spaces + Markdown repos work so well When you ask Copilot generic questions (“How should we log errors?” “What’s our API versioning approach?”), the model will often respond with reasonable defaults. But reasonable defaults are not the same as your standards. Copilot Spaces solve the...
item.dc:creator: mohashaikh
